questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of ISO/IEC 42001?
options: To define one mandatory AI model architecture
To provide a management system for responsible AI governance
To replace privacy and security standards
To eliminate the need for leadership oversight
questions.explanation: ISO/IEC 42001 is an organizational management-system standard for governing AI responsibly.
The standard follows the Plan-Do-Check-Act cycle.

questions.prompt: Which actions belong to leadership in an effective AIMS? Select all that apply.
options: Approve and communicate the AI policy
Assign accountability and reporting lines
Provide resources and visible sponsorship
Avoid management review once the system launches
Outsource all governance accountability to suppliers
questions.explanation: Leadership is expected to set direction, assign accountability, and sustain the AIMS with resources and review.

questions.prompt: Which AI-specific threat reconstructs sensitive training data through targeted queries?
options: Model theft
Model inversion
Direct injection
Data poisoning
questions.explanation: Model inversion infers sensitive training information from outputs or responses.

questions.prompt: Why is an AI policy considered a foundational governance control in ISO/IEC 42001?
options: Because it replaces risk assessment
Because it defines direction, expectations, and decision boundaries for responsible AI
Because it is only required for certification
Because it eliminates the need for human oversight
questions.explanation: The AI policy anchors direction, expectations, and governance intent.

questions.prompt: How does lifecycle management reduce AI-related operational and compliance risk?
options: By focusing only on procurement
By ensuring controls, approvals, testing, monitoring, and change discipline across the lifecycle
By removing the need for documentation
By making all models fully autonomous
questions.explanation: Lifecycle management reduces risk by applying controls and evidence requirements from requirements through monitoring and change.

questions.prompt: Why is data governance a distinct control area in an AI management system?
options: Because data quality, lineage, integrity, and appropriate use directly affect AI outcomes and risk
Because all AI systems use the same dataset
Because data governance is only a privacy issue
Because the model is more important than the data
questions.explanation: Data governance materially affects quality, fairness, reliability, privacy, and accountability.

questions.prompt: What governance risk can arise from third-party AI providers and external relationships?
options: No material risk if the contract is signed
Loss of accountability, weak oversight, dependency, and unmanaged supplier risk
Only infrastructure cost risk
Only reputational benefit
questions.explanation: Third-party AI relationships can introduce hidden dependencies and weaken oversight if not governed actively.

questions.prompt: Which activities belong mainly to the Check phase of PDCA? Select all that apply.
options: Monitoring and measurement
Internal audit
Management review
Initial scope definition
Corrective action planning
questions.explanation: Check is focused on monitoring, audit, review, and evaluation. Corrective action then feeds into Act.

questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes audit-ready evidence?
options: Only a signed executive memo
Records showing controls are defined, assigned, performed, and reviewed
A promise to document actions later
Only external certification marketing material
questions.explanation: Audit-ready evidence proves that governance works in practice, not only on paper.

questions.prompt: Which ISO standard establishes an information security management system? Enter digits only.
questions.explanation: ISO/IEC 27001 is the management-system standard for information security.

questions.prompt: What is the most credible implementation pattern for ISO/IEC 42001?
options: Treat it as a one-time documentation exercise
Establish scope, ownership, controls, evidence, review, and continual improvement
Adopt only supplier due diligence and ignore management review
Wait until an external audit forces action
questions.explanation: The standard works as a management system that must be operated, evidenced, reviewed, and improved over time.
